loop.observation: QA, workflow, and executable-knowledge evidence existed, but the relationship between loops, business claims, code paths, tests, docs, and evidence was still inferred by humans.
loop.hypothesis: A Loop Registry manifest can make loop ownership and business-claim bindings executable so an agent does not need to read prose before changing semantically bound files.
loop.execute: Verify loop entries, evidence-graph closure, claim file anchors, verifier source tokens, CI wiring, pre-commit wiring, generated-doc freshness, and optional changed-file co-change constraints.
loop.evaluate: The verifier fails when a loop omits observe/verify/evidence/freshness/fail conditions, when a claim has no code/doc/test/evidence binding, or when a claim-bound runtime file changes without bound proof changing too.
loop.retrospective: Harnesses can keep discovering failures while the registry decides which discoveries have become closed loops and which files must move together.

verifies: candidate count is visible in JSON evidence

evidence_graph.observation: local QA scenarios can be skipped or inferred without creating follow-up work.
evidence_graph.hypothesis: Every skipped, partial, failed, or inferred observation should become a closed_loop_candidate.
evidence_graph.change: evidence_gap_candidates derives candidates from local QA scenario outcomes.
evidence_graph.decision: partial status is expected while candidates remain.
evidence_graph.next_loop: promote one candidate into a system-owned verifier.

fails_when: inferred execution is counted as green

evidence_graph.observation: some QA actions still require human or Codex inference.
evidence_graph.hypothesis: Counting inferred execution explicitly prevents false green QA evidence.
evidence_graph.change: qaSystemScenario marks inferred execution as partial evidence.
evidence_graph.decision: remaining inferred ids are surfaced as candidates.
evidence_graph.next_loop: replace inferred entries with system-owned commands.

business_claims.text: Harness observations that are skipped, partial, failed, or inferred must become machine-readable closed-loop candidates instead of hidden follow-up work.
QA system evidence must report inferred execution separately and mark the audit partial until every loop has a system-owned verifier.
Daemon-owned QA harness work must never be committed or merged into teamswyg/riido-client; client worktrees are observed only.
Loop Registry entries must bind business claims to code paths, docs, verifier tokens, evidence names, workflow execution, and pre-commit execution.
